final_decision.exact_quote: Login concurrency has also increased from 100 to 1,000 users per minute, allowing even the largest campus drives to run as a single event. For greater scale needs, reach out to your account manager or support@hackerrank.com.
final_decision.justification: [ACTION_REQUEST] grounded generation; retrieval_score=0.74; verifier=pass; product_area_snapped=account->screen; exact_quote_source=deterministic_fallback
final_decision.response: To delete your HackerRank account, you will need to follow these steps: 1. Log in to your HackerRank account. 2. Go to your account settings. 3. Look for the option to delete your account. If you do not see this option, you may need to contact HackerRank support for further assistance. Please note that you cannot delete your account directly if you signed up using Google login without a password.

input.response: To delete your HackerRank account, you will need to follow these steps: 1. Log in to your HackerRank account. 2. Go to your account settings. 3. Look for the option to delete your account. If you do not see this option, you may need to contact HackerRank support for further assistance. Please note that you cannot delete your account directly if you signed up using Google login without a password.
